result.justification: No diagnostic or imaging procedures with documented abnormal or clinically decisive negative results performed within the last 12 months were found in the provided documents. The documents primarily contain diagnoses and clinical conditions without any detailed diagnostic procedure reports or imaging findings.

No inpatient hospitalizations were identified in the provided documents. The records primarily contain diagnoses and demographic information without any admission or discharge summaries, inpatient encounter metadata, or explicit statements of hospitalization.

documentSections.documentName: Continuity of Care Document (C-CDA R2.1) (Encounter date: 2025-10-22 11:00:00)

result.justification: The patient uses a walker and rolling equipment at home per social work note (10/31/2019), indicating fall risk. No anticoagulants are documented during hospitalization, but the patient is on multiple medications including furosemide and carvedilol which can contribute to hypotension and fall risk (medication lists from 2025). The inpatient notes from 10/30 to 11/04/2019 document oxygen use at 2 L/min on 10/30 and 10/31 but none on 11/01 and 11/04, indicating transient respiratory vulnerability. The patient was alert and oriented with family support, reducing cognitive safety concerns. No documented history of major bleeding or anticoagulant use was found. These risks are relevant for ongoing management to prevent falls, monitor respiratory status, and avoid bleeding complications.
result.medical_risks: fall propensity, medication-related bleeding risk, respiratory vulnerability

result.justification: The social history elements were extracted primarily from structured demographic fields and social history documentation in office visit and outpatient records dated between 2019 and 2025. Sex at birth, race, and ethnicity were explicitly noted in multiple demographic and social history documents. Tobacco status as a current user of chewing tobacco is consistent across multiple entries; no detailed quantification was documented. Alcohol use is consistently reported as none; recreational drug use is denied. No information on diet or exercise was found.

result.functional_barriers: Equipment used at home: walker, rolling
result.insurance_status: Medicaid waiver services for household chores
result.justification: Social work and case management notes from 10/31/2019 and 11/04/2019 provide detailed SDOH information including marital status as married, living situation with spouse and adult children in a house, use of walker equipment at home, transportation access via car and family/friend, and Medicaid waiver services indicating insurance coverage. No explicit documentation was found for education level, employment status, health literacy, financial, housing, food, utility insecurity, or mental health screening scores.
result.living_situation: Lives With: spouse; Sources of Support: spouse, adult child(ren); Living Arrangements: house
result.transportation_access: Transportation Available: car, family or friend will provide

result.Justification: The provided documents contain no information related to any vision-related visits, ophthalmology or optometry exams, or eye care evaluations for the patient. There are no notes, imaging reports, or exam summaries referencing visual acuity, retinal status, diabetic eye disease, or glaucoma screening.

result.other_pertinent_information: The patient is a 72-year-old female with a history of right renal mass diagnosed in 2019, confirmed as renal cell carcinoma, for which she underwent a right hand-assisted laparoscopic radical nephrectomy on 10/30/2019 without complications. She has a history of essential hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, gastroesophageal reflux disease without esophagitis, and familial hypercholesterolemia. She is status post hysterectomy and cesarean section with one vaginal delivery. Socially, she is married, lives with her spouse, has adult children, uses a cane for ambulation, and has arranged for home health services post-hospital discharge. No recent echocardiogram, mammogram, Pap smear, or colonoscopy were documented.
